growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

up.method: dFBA prediction (consumed, but medium also supplies them so not counted as absolute exchange); no numeric flux reported

sec.method: dFBA model prediction; no numeric flux reported
dFBA model prediction; no numeric flux reported
dFBA model prediction; no numeric flux reported

growth_records.cite: Wang et al. Formation of a constructed microbial community in a nutrient-rich environment indicates bacterial interspecific competition. mSystems (2024). DOI:10.1128/msystems.00006-24. [PMC11019790]
growth_records.snippet: strain AP49 is predicted to secrete niacin, inosine, and NH4+... Although Sphingobium sp. AP49 is predicted to consume these amino acids, they were not considered as absolute interspecies exchanges since the medium environment also contains
growth_records.notes: mu_max measured (CFU + qPCR give similar results for AP49) but numeric value only in Fig 1d, not in text; growth rate not curatable. Secretion (niacin, inosine, NH4+) and amino-acid uptake are dFBA predictions without numeric fluxes; captured rate=null. R2A composition not stated. 30C, 200 rpm, batch monoculture.
